stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump says Tehran wants deal as US blockade in Hormuz begins
stories.summary: Iran accuses US of committing piracy as thousands rally in Tehran against the blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Oil jumps and Japan stocks slip as Trump threatens Hormuz blockade
Ukraine war briefing: Hungary’s new leader says he would ask Putin to end the killing in Ukraine
stories.summary: Péter Magyar would ‘talk to Russian president, but won’t initiate contact’; Ukraine welcomes defeat of Orbán. What we know on day 1,511Péter Magyar, Hungary’s new leader, said he would ask Vladimir Putin to end the killing in Ukraine if they speak, and plans to review Hungary’s Russian energy contracts and renegotiate them if needed. Magyar said he would talk to the Russian president, but won’t initiate contact. “If Vladimir Putin calls, I’ll pic

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan arrive in Australia for four-day tour
stories.summary: It is the couple's first visit to Australia since 2018 when they were working royals.

stories.headline: Korean Air Shares Up on Earnings Surprise Despite Jet Fuel Fears
stories.summary: Korean Air Lines Co. shares climbed after South Korea’s largest flag carrier delivered a first-quarter earnings surprise, defying mounting concerns about jet fuel shortages and surging costs amid the Middle East crisis.

stories.headline: BlackRock’s Asia Private Credit Fund Sees China Borrower Default
stories.summary: A BlackRock Inc. private credit fund in Asia has suffered the first default by a borrower in its portfolio after a Chinese company failed to repay a loan, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
stories.summary: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.

stories.headline: Tony Gonzales, a Texas Congressman, Says He Will Resign
stories.summary: The third-term Republican lawmaker is accused of coercing a staff member into a sexual relationship.

stories.headline: Barclays keeping eye on volatile private credit market, CEO says
Australia’s Sovereign Wealth Fund Reviews Some Investment Roles
stories.summary: Australia’s A$335 billion ($238 billion) sovereign wealth fund is reviewing 10 positions, including some investment roles, as part of a cost-cutting drive.

stories.headline: European Natural Gas Falls Amid Limited Trading in Asian Hours
stories.summary: European natural gas prices slipped in tepid early trading during Asian hours.

stories.headline: Colombia Credit Chief Eyes $4 Billion Buyback of External Bonds
stories.summary: Colombia is planning to repurchase some $4 billion of its bonds, according to public credit director Javier Cuellar.

stories.headline: U.S. Military Strikes Another Boat in Pacific, Killing 2
stories.summary: The attack was the 49th since the campaign against vessels in the Caribbean and the eastern Pacific started in early September.

stories.headline: Australia news live: Taylor claims people who migrate from liberal democracies likelier to share Australian values than those from ‘other places’
stories.summary: The opposition leader said Australia is ‘changing for the worse’ due to declining immigration standardsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastSydney is getting its first new Roman Catholic Cathedral in more than a century, part of a massive 7.7-hectare integrated precinct in the northern suburb of Waitara. Irish architect Niall McLaughlin, winner of the Royal Institute of British Architects’ Royal Gold Medal, will lead the des

stories.headline: SoftBank taps Honda, other manufacturers for input on physical AI
Japan's Takaichi targets spring 2027 for advancing constitutional revisions
Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.summary: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: US, Iran Eye More Talks as US Blockade Begins
stories.summary: President Donald Trump began a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a move intended to raise pressure on Tehran, even as the two countries are weighing another round of talks in hopes of cementing a longer-term ceasefire. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Israel summons Italy's ambassador over comments on 'unacceptable attacks' on Lebanon
stories.summary: Lebanon was pulled into the Middle East war when Iran-backed group Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel on 2 March, days after the opening salvo of US-Israeli strikes killed Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei.

stories.headline: Israel kills three Palestinians in separate Gaza strikes
stories.summary: Two Palestinians were killed in separate strikes in southern Gaza on Monday, according to officials at Nasser hospital.

stories.headline: Oil Declines as US, Iran Weigh More Talks With Blockade in Place
stories.summary: Oil dropped on signs Washington and Tehran may revive peace talks following the start of a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: What the U.S. Blockade of Iran and the Strait of Hormuz Might Look Like
stories.summary: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.

stories.headline: Israel needs to stop encouraging settler violence, Palestinian FM says
stories.summary: Palestinian foreign minister told Euronews Israel should protect Palestinians in the West Bank as the occupier, and that instead the government supports settler violence. She also said the Iran war had further marginalised Palestinian aspirations to statehood.

stories.headline: Brazil’s former spy chief who fled country arrested by ICE agents in US
stories.summary: Alexandre Ramagem fled country after he was sentenced to 16 years for his role in plotting military coup in BrazilWhen Brazil’s former president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to nearly 30 years in prison for an attempted coup, six other members of his cabinet were also found guilty and all began serving their sentences – except for one.Days before the verdict, Alexandre Ramagem, Bolsonaro’s former spy chief, fled by car to Guyana and boarded a fli

stories.headline: Trump says Iran wants ‘peace deal’ but insists on ‘no nukes’
stories.summary: Donald Trump says Iran wants to make a peace deal “very badly” after talks in Islamabad ended without agreement.

stories.headline: Pope Leo XIV says he has 'no fear' of Trump administration
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV said he has no intention to debate with Donald Trump after the US president criticised him over his comments on the Iran war. "I have no fear of the Trump administration nor of speaking out loudly about the message of the gospel," the pope told reporters aboard the papal plane.

stories.headline: UN experts slam attacks on Gaza shelters, forced displacement in West Bank
stories.summary: Panel details incidents of violence by Israeli forces and settlers against displaced Palestinians in Gaza and West Bank.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

stories.headline: Nigeria Air Force orders investigation after dozens killed in airstrike
stories.summary: There are reports that more than 100 people were killed, although the authorities have not commented.

stories.headline: Eight to be awarded for bravery in Bondi Junction stabbing
stories.summary: Six people were killed and ten injured after a man went on a stabbing rampage in an Australian mall.

stories.headline: Blue Owl Private Credit Fund Raises $400 Million in Bond Market
stories.summary: A Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit fund raised $400 million from bond investors Monday, marking the first deal of its kind in over a month, as worries about AI-disrupted software bets and looser lending standards have roiled the once red-hot industry.

stories.headline: Wave of $15 Billion US IPOs Runs Headlong Into War’s New Phase
stories.summary:
Investment bankers are hitting the road to raise more than $15 billion across initial public offerings in the coming weeks, nervously eyeing market volatility as a standoff with Iran threatens a fragile ceasefire.
The success of a string of deals, led this week by a $2.23 billion IPO for ventilation and filtration systems provider Madison Air Solutions Corp., will be closely watched. Bankers are looking to put choppy performance for recent debuta

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
stories.summary: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.

stories.headline: 'We cannot ask any country to give up its territory,' Magyar says on Ukraine
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Viktor Orbán had largely centred his electoral campaign on Ukraine and Volodymyr Zelenskyy, blaming Kyiv for the Druzhba pipeline blockade to accusations of meddling in Hungarian elections. Will things change with Magyar in power?

stories.headline: Philippines accuses China of using cyanide to poison South China Sea atoll
stories.summary: Officials warn cyanide could harm marine life and weaken the reef supporting a grounded warship.

stories.headline: Starmer announces talks to address the Strait of Hormuz crisis
stories.summary: Starmer has announced talks to be held this week and co-hosted with France, aiming at diplomatic end to the war on Iran

stories.headline: Up to 200 civilians killed in Nigeria after air force 'misfire' on market, sources say
stories.summary: Such misfires are common in Nigeria, where the military often conducts air raids to battle armed groups who control vast forest enclaves.

stories.headline: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
stories.summary: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

stories.headline: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
stories.summary: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.headline: Most Emerging Assets Gain After Hormuz Blockade Spurs Oil Spike
stories.summary: Most emerging-market currencies climbed on Monday after oil retreated from session highs when President Donald Trump said Iran had reached out seeking a deal, even as the US began enforcing a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Prosecutors Investigate ICE Agents Who Detained ChongLy Scott Thao in Minnesota
stories.summary: ChongLy Scott Thao, a naturalized U.S. citizen, was briefly detained during a federal immigration crackdown in Minnesota in January.

stories.headline: World War II Veterans Say NATO Is Imperfect but Necessary
stories.summary: President Trump’s stance on NATO has upended the norms that the service of World War II veterans helped to establish. Some say the partnership could be revamped for modern times.

stories.headline: Judge dismisses Trump’s lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein drawing
stories.summary: A federal judge on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by President Donald Trump that claimed that the Wall Street Journal defamed him by publishing a drawing of a naked woman it said Trump contributed to a birthday gift for Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.headline: And the election winner is … the candidate who can afford Africa’s soaring nomination fees
stories.summary: Presidential elections in Djibouti and Benin at the weekend highlighted how a costly electoral system is reshaping democracyAlexis Mohamed would have loved to stand against his former boss. A longtime adviser to Djibouti’s president, Ismail Omar Guelleh, Mohamed resigned last September, citing democratic regression in the country.But at the election at the weekend, Mohamed was not on the ballot. Now outside the country, he says he cannot return h

stories.headline: Colombia to cull up to 80 hippos descended from Pablo Escobar zoo
stories.summary: Dozens of feral pachyderms linked to drug kingpin to be killed because of threat to native species and villagersColombian officials have authorized a plan to cull dozens of hippos descended from animals brought to the country in the 1980s by Pablo Escobar, after the feral beasts displaced native species and threatened local villagers.The environment minister, Irene Vélez, said the decision was reached because other methods to control their popula

stories.headline: What’s behind Viktor Orban’s defeat in Hungary’s election?
stories.summary: Hungary's opposition has defeated the prime minister by a landslide.

stories.headline: Benin’s Wadagni wins presidential election with landslide 94% of votes
stories.summary: The incoming president faces serious security issues in the north of the country and challenges to living standards.

stories.headline: Is Magyar’s election win the end of the EU’s troubles with Hungary?
stories.summary: Orban's exit signals potential end to Hungary's EU isolation and opens door for diplomatic and economic collaboration.

stories.headline: Judge dimisses Trump's lawsuit against WSJ over reporting on ties to Epstein
stories.summary: The judge, who was appointed by former president Barack Obama, gave Trump the opportunity to amend his complaint and refile the suit by 27 April.

stories.headline: Péter Magyar accuses outgoing foreign minister of destroying confidential documents
stories.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister-elect Peter Magyar has accused outgoing Foreign Minister Péter Szijjártó of shredding confidential sanctions-related documents at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, citing insider sources during a press conference in Budapest on Monday. The ministry has not yet responded.

stories.headline: Volatility Stymies Asia Carry Even as Returns Rise, ANZ Says
stories.summary: Elevated currency swings make carry trades unattractive in emerging Asia, according to Australia & New Zealand Banking Group, even with returns at the highest levels in nearly four years.

stories.headline: Asian Stocks Rise on Iran Deal Hopes, Oil Falls: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian stocks advanced and crude oil extended declines as President Donald Trump raised hopes for an Iran deal, even as a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz took effect.

stories.headline: Antofagasta Signals Interest in Joining Argentina’s Copper Rush
stories.summary: Antofagasta Plc is taking an early look at opportunities in Argentina as sweeping policy changes revive interest in one of the world’s most promising new copper frontiers, Chief Executive Officer Iván Arriagada said in an interview.

stories.headline: Pakistan Weighs ‘All Options’ to Sustain Reserves After UAE Move
stories.summary: Pakistan is considering financing from both countries and banks as options to help maintain foreign-exchange reserve levels amid soaring oil prices, Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb said, after the United Arab Emirates asked for the full repayment of a $3 billion loan.

stories.headline: Raizen’s Creditors Said to Ask for 90% Equity Stake in Debt Swap
stories.summary: Raízen SA bondholders and bank creditors are asking for a stake of as much as 90% of the company in exchange for 45% of its debt in a restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Chevron Expands Its Venezuela Oil Operations Through Asset Swap
stories.summary: Chevron Corp. agreed to an asset swap with Venezuela that will significantly expand its oil operations in the country after remaining there for decades amid political and economic turmoil.

stories.headline: New Zealand Dairy Farmers Pocket Billions From Huge Capital Payout
stories.summary: New Zealand dairy farmers are about to get a record multi-billion dollar, tax-free payout. They may need every cent of it.

stories.headline: Peru extends voting for president into Monday after chaos at polling stations
stories.summary: Lack of ballot papers and defective computers disrupt election that Keiko Fujimori appears to be leadingPeruvians will have to wait at least until the end of Monday to know the result of the presidential election held on Sunday, after the voting process descended into chaos in some polling stations due to a lack of ballot papers or defective computers.In an unprecedented move, Peru’s electoral agency ONPE announced on Sunday night that it would e

stories.summary: Premier Chris Minns says policy will give road users a ‘real alternative’ that’s cheaper than petrolFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastElectric truck development and building more stations to charge them will be core pillars of a state’s revamped electric vehicle strategy designed to ease the pressure of rising fuel costs.The New South Wales government unveiled its 2026

stories.headline: Bosses of Santos, Woodside, Chevron and Shell asked to give evidence to Greens-led gas tax inquiry
stories.summary: Labor is under pressure to impose a new 25% export tax amid soaring prices from the global fuel shockFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe bosses of resources giants including Santos, Woodside, Chevron and Shell could be compelled to face an inquiry into export tax settings, as the Greens ramp up pressure on Labor before the budget.The chief executives of the companies

stories.headline: New White House report has plans to fix US housing shortage of 10 million
stories.summary: A new report lays out a blueprint for how more home construction would help the middle class and the overall economy.

stories.headline: Indian workers protest over rising living cost
stories.summary: Police have scaled up enforcement in the Indian city of Noida as a factory workers' protest turns violent.

stories.headline: Germany marks 69th birthday of zoo’s oldest gorilla
stories.summary: Berlin Zoo celebrates Fatou, a 69‑year‑old western lowland gorilla, highlighting how specialised care in captivity can extend the lives of endangered species.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
stories.summary: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

stories.headline: Anthony Albanese urges Israel to stop Lebanon attacks that intensified during Middle East ceasefire
stories.summary: PM tells Guardian Australia Hezbollah should cease reprisals and confirms Australia’s military surveillance aircraft will remain in regionFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAustralia’s prime minister, Anthony Albanese, has urged Israel to stop its attacks on Lebanon and raised concern over its intensified military campaign on Beirut and the country’s south after the cea

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.headline: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.headline: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.headline: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.
